title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Acting on a request from Miss Marple, Detective Inspector Clouseau meets with Dermot Clarke to investigate the matter of "Charlotte." During their discussion, Mrs. Bantry and Demott Cluttercuck evaluate potential motives for the death of Heather Badcock, concluding that she possessed little wealth to inherit and had no known enemies or history of blackmailing others. While Dr. Mortimer suggests Heather may have possessed knowledge harmful to others, Mrs. Bantry remains skeptical.

Mrs. Bantry describes a specific expression she witnessed during a reception held on a landing platform, which she refers to as the "Charlotte look"—an expression of profound, chilling horror inspired by Tennyson's poetry. She observed this look on Marina Gregg. While Heather Badcock was speaking to Marina, Marina appeared to be staring past her toward a hallway or a painting, as if she had seen something terrible. Mrs. Bantry recalls several attendees at the event, including the mayor and his wife, a red-haired man she suspected might be a reporter named Calbraith, an imposing dark-skinned man accompanied by a glamorous actress, General Barnstaple, the Grays from the farm, and a pretentious girl from London carrying a large camera.

In a separate inquiry, Cornish reports to Cluttercuck that Mrs. Badcock had no enemies, no suspicious affairs, and a stable marriage. Regarding her husband, Arthur Badcock, Cornish notes that Arthur works at the Bidell Russell real estate agency alongside colleagues Flore West and Miss Glendale. Additionally, Connington mentions that an attractive widow living next door to the Badcocks, described as having "gypsy beauty," has been caring for Arthur and appears determined to marry him.
